reasoning: The question asks if Anish Giri's Elo rating on Wikipedia's 'FIDE rankings' page will be at least 1% higher on 2026-03-22 than on 2026-03-15. FIDE publishes classical ratings only once per month, on the 1st of each month. The Wikipedia page reflects these static monthly lists, updating shortly after the 1st of each month (the current page clearly states "ranked on 1 March 2026"). Since there are no new FIDE publications between March 15 and March 22, the listed rating on Wikipedia is overwhelmingly likely to remain completely identical on both dates. Furthermore, even if live ratings were tracked, a 1% increase equates to gaining roughly 28 Elo points, an astronomical jump for a 2750+ rated player in an entire month, let alone a 7-day span — plus, Giri's next major event (the 2026 Candidates Tournament) does not begin until late March anyway. Therefore, the chance of this resolving YES is effectively zero.
